hints.text: Bollards: **red strip wrapping around the top & whole back** + thin yellow/white reflector — unique to NZ
hints.uniq_note: Australia's red reflector stops mid-bollard instead of wrapping.

hints.text: Plates white w/ black text (mostly white through blur; custom plates can show blue/black edges)
Most common pole: concrete w/ **one long indent** + small silver **possum guards**; round wooden & 'holey' concrete poles too
hints.uniq_note: Indented concrete + possum guard = NZ pole signature.

hints.text: Give-way signs: white border + **RED text**; **give-way triangles painted on the road**

shared_with.note: AU: black text, no painted triangles.

hints.text: Directional/warning signs on **white posts**; warning = yellow diamonds; **blue street signs** dominate (esp. rural) — Australia almost never uses blue
Outer lines white (yellow DASHED outer in towns; none on small roads); inner = single dashed white or **unbroken double yellow**

shared_with.note: AU has no yellow inner lines (outside snow areas).

hints.text: Chevrons **black-on-yellow w/ a thin black border** (AU: same colours, NO border); green direction signs w/ highway number in a **red crest**; brown signs → landmarks; **bridge markers** on most paved roads (yellow = river/bridge name)
hints.uniq_note: The bordered chevron + red-crest highway shields are quick NZ locks.

hints.text: **2-digit state highways numbered N→S** (bollards sometimes show the number); phone = 2-digit area code + 7 digits (02 = mobile); **Fonterra farm-sign first digit = region**; unique bus stops → Auckland/Wellington/Christchurch; **rubbish-bin council logos** ID cities (green lids ≈ Christchurch)

bullets: Waikato/Northland: drier rolling hills (Northland very hilly, lots of Gen2 + gravel)
West Coast + Tasman: temperate rainforest, rugged, dark compact greenery
Southland/Otago flats: cold alpine slopes w/ stunted vegetation
Southern Alps: big snowy range down the South Island
Hawke's Bay: dry golden hills, sunny Gen4 w/ white car
Manawatū-Whanganui: bright sandy cliffs (also Taranaki/Waikato)
Queenstown area: teal glacial lakes
Mt Taranaki: smooth lone cone in flat surroundings
Canterbury plains: flat fields + tall hedge rows, mountains west (even taller hedges → Tauranga/Bay of Plenty)
Vineyards: Marlborough + Hawke's Bay
Corn: almost only North Island; flax in neat rows → Southland
Olive-brown square possum guards → Marlborough
Long cylindrical silver guards → Southland (rarer W-Otago)
Holey poles → Southland + around Auckland
Square un-indented concrete poles → Southland (+ Otago/Taranaki/Manawatū)
Off-centre crossbars → Otago, Marlborough, Wellington, Taranaki
Middle insulator on a tall metal bar → Auckland/Waikato border
White-painted crossbar middles → E-Waikato + Whanganui (+ pockets)
Yellow cow-tag on poles → Gisborne (+ N-Hawke's Bay)
Danger-triangle warning sticker → Hawke's Bay, Taupo↔Rotorua, N-Waikato, S of Auckland
Black 5-digit sticker → West Coast/Tasman/S-Canterbury/S-Northland
Black 6-digit sticker + small square → most of the North Island (starts 3/4) + Southland/Otago (starts 8)
Orange 'DANGER LIVE WIRES' paint → Timaru area
Small black horizontal number plate → Taranaki + Wellington; white version → parts of the North Island

hints.text: **More region metas**: solid white outers w/o middle → Gisborne (+ N-Hawke's Bay; Wellington = narrower); green-striped bollards → S-Canterbury (+ E-Southland); black-striped bollards → Wellington (old Gen3); fences: tight posts → North Island + N-South Island, wide gaps → South Island + Taranaki, thin metal posts → almost only South Island; short wooden fence-post extensions → Hawke's Bay, long → Southland/Hawke's Bay
**Car metas**: white-blue front car → mostly North Island (occasionally Canterbury/Marlborough); blurred white-blue-white striped car → South Island + around/north of Auckland

**Town/city street-sign graphics** for the north + south (clickable); **Wellington** = super hilly; Lower/Upper Hutt = wide green SW-NE valley; Blenheim = hill ranges north + south; Christchurch = 2011-earthquake scars (broken roads in 2012 coverage, empty ex-house districts later)

bullets: Stewart Island: only around Oban, boat-filled bay, Gen3 only
Tongariro NP: low volcanic vegetation, cloud-capped Ruapehu/Tongariro
Mt Ruapehu (Whakapapa): dark volcanic bare landscape
SH85 (Becks↔Morisson, Otago): light snow coverage
Fruit farms under green netting → Tauranga (+ Gisborne)
Banks Peninsula (Akaroa): rocky grass hills + tree patches + gorse
Marlborough Sounds: forested mountains from the water (Gen4)
Otago Peninsula: cabbage trees + yellow bushes + cone pines + flax
SH73 (Arthur's Pass): dry gravel riverbed + black pickup w/ 2 ladders ahead
SH94 (Milford Sound): dense red-beech forest + snowy peaks
SH80: N-S glacial valley, Tasman River + Lake Pukaki east
Pirongia (long ridge w/ 2 bumps) + Kakepuku (cone) seen together — Waikato
Putauaki volcano (Bay of Plenty): cut-off 'frog' top
